timeline.label: Filed general rate case seeking ~$179M annualized incremental revenue (plus ~$32M infrastructure surcharges pending approval)
Closed acquisition of regulated water/wastewater systems from Nexus Regulated Utilities (~$200M rate base)
Shareholders approved stock-for-stock merger with Essential Utilities; expected close Q1 2027 pending regulatory approvals
Announced merger agreement with Essential Utilities to combine largest U.S. investor-owned water utilities
FY2025: operating revenue $5.14B (+9.7% YoY); net income $1.11B; diluted EPS $5.69; operating cash flow $2.06B; capex $3.13B
Declared annual dividend $2.83 per share; quarterly cash dividend $0.8275
FY2024 operating revenue $4.68B; diluted EPS $5.39; continued infrastructure investment and rate-base growth
FY2023 operating revenue $4.23B; regulated businesses ~93% of revenue
Completed sale of non-regulated contract operations group; refocused on regulated water/wastewater utility model
IPO and spin from RWE; became largest U.S. investor-owned water and wastewater utility by population served

industry.structure: U.S. investor-owned water and wastewater is a fragmented, regulation-heavy oligopoly: American Water serves ~3.6 million customers across 14 states with local monopoly franchises. Pricing is set by state PUCs balancing affordability and allowed return on rate-base capital. Essential, American States Water, and California Water Service are primary IOU peers.
industry.share_shift: AWK is gaining scale through tuck-in municipal/system acquisitions (Nexus, privatization bids) and the pending Essential merger would roughly double the combined footprint. Smaller municipal systems continue selling to IOUs as capex needs exceed local tax capacity.
industry.trend: Aging pipe replacement, lead-service-line rules, and climate resilience are driving elevated capital spending and infrastructure surcharge mechanisms. Consolidation among IOUs is accelerating; financing costs and regulatory lag remain the main swing factors for equity returns.
